description: Lead merge profile - based on Salesforce Record Merging Runbook with converted lead handling

relatedObjects.description: Tasks related to this Lead (polymorphic WhoId field)
Events related to this Lead (polymorphic WhoId field)
Lead field history (read-only, cannot be reparented)

convertedLead.description: Runbook: Cannot merge two converted leads. At least one must be unconverted.
convertedLead.rule: IF master.IsConverted=true AND duplicate.IsConverted=true THEN BLOCK_MERGE
convertedLead.cliImplementation: Query IsConverted field, validate at least one is false before merge
convertedLead.recommendation: If both converted, deduplicate before conversion for best results

campaignMembers.description: Runbook: Salesforce automatically handles duplicate campaign memberships (one per Campaign)
campaignMembers.cliImplementation: Reparent CampaignMember records, Salesforce deduplicates automatically

leadConversion.description: Merging doesn't undo conversion. Master status preserved.
leadConversion.note: If merging converted lead into unconverted, the unconverted lead remains unconverted

fieldResolution.description: Runbook rule: Master values win unless null. Preserve lead qualification data from duplicate.

validation.description: Comprehensive validation: permissions, converted lead status (cannot merge two converted leads)

performance.description: Optimizations: explicit field selection, metadata caching, parallel related record updates

runbookCompliance.cliImplementation: sf data query (check IsConverted) + sf data update + sf data delete
runbookCompliance.fieldResolutionRule: Master values win unless null (Runbook default)
runbookCompliance.relatedRecordHandling: Automatic reparenting including polymorphic WhoId fields and CampaignMember

specialCaseHandling.convertedLeads: BLOCK merge if both IsConverted=true. Error: 'Cannot merge two converted leads'
specialCaseHandling.campaignMembers: Reparent to master, Salesforce handles deduplication per Campaign automatically
specialCaseHandling.leadConversion: Master's conversion status is preserved (doesn't inherit from duplicate)

runbookCompliance.runbookReference: Leads - Using SOAP API (Merge Leads) section

notes: Runbook: Cannot merge two converted leads (at least one must be unconverted)
Runbook: Lead conversion vs merge are separate operations (merge doesn't create Account/Contact)
Best practice: Merge leads BEFORE conversion to avoid converted lead complications
CampaignMember records: Salesforce automatically prevents duplicate memberships per Campaign
LeadHistory: Field history records cannot be reparented (read-only tracking)
If merging is needed post-conversion, prefer unconverted lead as master for clean state
Use queryAll() to audit merged leads later: query IsDeleted=true with MasterRecordId
